name: Air Canada

carryOn.notes: Standard and higher fares: 1 carry-on + 1 personal item. Basic fare: personal item only.

economy.notes: Included in Standard and higher fares. Basic does not include checked baggage.

fees.firstBag: Included in Standard+; Basic fare: CAD $30–50

batteryRules.lithiumIon: Spare lithium-ion batteries permitted in carry-on only
batteryRules.spareBatteries: Carry-on only. Up to 100Wh unrestricted. 101–160Wh with airline approval. Over 160Wh prohibited.
batteryRules.powerBankLimit: 100Wh unrestricted; up to 160Wh with Air Canada approval
batteryRules.laptops: Allowed in carry-on. Remove at security screening.

restrictedItems: Smart bags with non-removable batteries exceeding 160Wh

faqs.question: Does Air Canada Basic fare include a carry-on bag?
faqs.answer: No. Air Canada Basic fare only includes one personal item under the seat. A carry-on bag in the overhead bin is not included and will incur extra fees.

faqs.question: What are Air Canada's checked bag fees?
faqs.answer: Standard and higher fares include one 23 kg checked bag. Basic fare passengers pay approximately CAD $30–50 for the first bag. Premium Economy includes two bags; Business Class also includes two bags at 32 kg each.

faqs.question: Can I bring a power bank on Air Canada?
faqs.answer: Power banks must be in carry-on luggage only — not checked bags. Power banks up to 100Wh are permitted without approval. Banks between 101–160Wh require Air Canada approval before flying.
